tweets: Stop trusting Claude Code. Seriously. Hot take, but hear me out. Building a local SEO SaaS in Rails for months, Claude Code is a real unlock. But nobody talks enough about how it quietly ignores your instructions with full confidence. #AI #buildinpublic
Not hallucinations. Not made up APIs. The subtle stuff. I tell it a specific date format. Diff looks perfect. Tests green. A week later half the CSV exports are in a different format. #AIcoding #SaaS
I put never use double hyphens in my project instructions. Explicit. Loaded into every single session. Three days later, double hyphens littered across my commit messages. #AIcoding #devtools
I give it a precise spec for queue backpressure. I get back close enough. In a way that only breaks under real load. Cool. Love that for me. #buildinpublic #SaaS

This isn't Claude being dumb. It's Claude being averaged. The model reaches for the common pattern instead of your specific one. Its confidence is identical either way. That's the dangerous part. #AI #LLM
What actually saved me. Read every diff like the thing never saw your instructions. Don't ask did you do X. Ask show me where you did X. #AIcoding #devtools
Run it end to end. Tests lie. Tests really lie. If a change looks right but feels a little lucky, it's almost always wrong. #buildinpublic #AIcoding

Treat Claude Code like a genuinely talented junior engineer who's slightly too sure of themselves. Pair with it, don't autopilot with it. The speed is real. The drift is also real. Trust but verify. Every. Single. Time. #AIcoding #buildinpublic #SaaS #devtools
